spec.goal: Harden same-thread continuation against tracker read-after-write lag without reopening the merged XY-157 lane.
success_criteria: Turn-1 continuation validation accepts a locally successful startup transition even when the immediate tracker reread is stale.
Continuation-blocking local stop writes remain blocking until tracker rereads confirm the issue is active again.
Targeted tests cover stale reread cases for both startup-transition and stop-write paths.
Repo-native verification passes on the repaired lane.
constraints: Do not rewrite XY-157 history or reopen the merged PR; land this as a follow-up fix lane.
Preserve the conservative terminal-path enforcement added in XY-157.
Do not touch the root checkout's unrelated WORKFLOW.md change.

tasks.title: Pin the stale-reread semantics
tasks.objective: Identify the smallest safe contract for local successful tracker writes versus stale tracker rereads so the repair closes the late review without reopening the old continuation holes.
inputs: Late review threads on PR #18 discussion_r2958265829 and discussion_r2958265833
Current orchestrator continuation guard and tracker tool bridge logic on origin/main
outputs: A concrete repair shape for startup-transition lag and stop-write lag
Target test list for the stale-reread cases
verification: Cross-check the chosen repair against the current code paths and existing continuation tests

tasks.title: Implement lag hardening and tests
tasks.objective: Apply the runtime changes and add targeted tests for stale tracker rereads after local tracker writes.
inputs: Current continuation and tracker bridge tests
outputs: Code changes in the continuation guard / tracker bridge
Targeted tests for stale startup-transition and stop-write rereads
verification: Targeted cargo test coverage for the new stale-reread scenarios

tasks.title: Run gates and record repair evidence
tasks.objective: Run repo-native verification, update the saved plan state/evidence, and determine whether the lane is ready for review.
outputs: Updated plan state reflecting execution results

triggers: The repair needs a broader contract change than a local post-merge fix.
Tests show the stale-reread cases require changing the lifecycle or tracker tool surface.
Review feedback implies new module or workflow boundaries.

evidence: 2026-03-19: Created XY-183 as a follow-up fix after PR #18 merged and then received a final Codex review pass at 2026-03-19T07:14:42Z with two new read-after-write lag findings.

2026-03-19: Pinned the repair shape to two local signals: turn-1 continuation now honors a locally successful startup transition when the immediate tracker reread still reflects the pre-write snapshot, and continuation-blocking local stop writes stay blocking until tracker rereads confirm the issue is active again.

2026-03-19: Added stale-reread regression coverage in orchestrator::tests::continuation_guard_allows_turn_one_after_local_startup_transition_on_stale_reread plus tracker_tool_bridge stale-active stop-write tests for both opt-out labels and non-In-Progress transitions.

2026-03-19: Post-commit full-harness verification exposed one remaining shared-state flake in state::tests::shared_dispatch_slots_honor_configured_limit_across_process_local_stores; root cause was failed slot contenders relying on implicit issue-claim guard drop instead of explicit unlock on the false-return path.

2026-03-19: Hardened StateStore shared-lock cleanup by explicitly unlocking failed contender issue-claim guards before returning false and adding deterministic state regressions for failed_shared_slot_attempt_releases_issue_claim_before_retry plus shared_issue_claim_reopens_same_issue_after_clear_across_process_local_stores.

2026-03-19: Codex review on PR #19 identified a daemon-handoff regression: clear_lease must not explicitly unlock handoff-cloned issue or dispatch guards because clone_issue_claim_for_child and clone_dispatch_slot_for_child duplicate those fds into the daemon child.

2026-03-19: Refined the shared-lock repair to tag guards with handoff_cloned metadata so clear_lease explicitly unlocks ordinary guards for deterministic release but preserves handoff-cloned guards until the last inherited holder drops them; failed contender issue claims still unlock immediately on false-return.

2026-03-19: Added adopted_issue_claim_blocks_same_issue_after_parent_clears_local_guard to lock the same-issue handoff case: with two shared slots available, parent clear_lease now drops only its local cloned guard while the adopted child still blocks reacquisition of the same issue claim.

2026-03-19: Verification passed: cargo test continuation_guard_allows_turn_one_after_local_startup_transition_on_stale_reread -- --nocapture; cargo test turn_classification_rejects_ -- --nocapture; cargo test --all-features failed_shared_slot_attempt_releases_issue_claim_before_retry -- --nocapture; cargo test --all-features shared_dispatch_slots_honor_configured_limit_across_process_local_stores -- --nocapture; cargo test --all-features shared_issue_claim_reopens_same_issue_after_clear_across_process_local_stores -- --nocapture; cargo test --all-features adopted_dispatch_slot_blocks_after_parent_releases_local_guard -- --nocapture; cargo test --all-features adopted_issue_claim_blocks_same_issue_after_parent_clears_local_guard -- --nocapture; cargo make fmt-check; cargo make lint; cargo make test; git diff --check.
